# AI-Me Discovery Manifest Specification — Full Documentation

> Open specification for machine-readable discovery manifests — one-fetch, site-wide discovery for AI agents.

## What is AI-Me?

AI-Me defines a JSON document served at `/.well-known/ai-me.json` that provides a machine-readable discovery manifest for any website. It gives AI agents a single-fetch, site-wide view of who the site owner is, what they offer, and how to engage — with canonical pages, offerings, pricing models, and calls-to-action in a strict, validatable shape.

The format is JSON, designed to be easy to generate, validate, and cache. It is complementary to:
- **llms.txt** — human-readable guidance for LLMs
- **schema.org** — page-level structured data for search engines
- **MCP** — tool and data integration for AI applications
- **A2A** — agent-to-agent interoperability

## Specification Version

Current version: **v0.1 (Draft)**
Specification URL: https://spec.ai-me.dev/spec
JSON Schema: https://spec.ai-me.dev/schemas/ai-me.schema.json

## Discovery

A conforming site MUST serve the discovery manifest at: `/.well-known/ai-me.json`
A site MAY also serve identical content at: `/ai-manifest.json`
The well-known location is canonical per RFC 8615.

## Required Fields

Every discovery manifest MUST include:
- `schema_version` — string, must be "0.1" for this draft
- `name` — public-facing name of the organization or project
- `website` — absolute URL of the site's primary web presence
- `summary` — brief, plain-language description
- `canonical_pages` — object with at least `home` and `contact` (both absolute URLs)
- `last_updated` — ISO 8601 date or date-time

## Recommended Fields

- `legal_name` — registered legal entity name
- `logo` — absolute URL to a square image (min 256×256, PNG or SVG)
- `languages` — array of BCP 47 language tags
- `regions` — array of geographic regions served
- `category` — array of descriptive categories

## Offerings

Optional array of product/service objects. Each offering includes:
- `name`, `type` (service|product|subscription|api|tool|content|open_source|other), `description`
- `pricing` with `model` (free|freemium|fixed|tiered|usage|quote|contact_sales|daily_rate|open_source), `currency` (ISO 4217), `url`
- `cta` with `label` and `url`

## Related Resources

The `related` object links to complementary protocols:
- `schema_org` — URL to site-level JSON-LD
- `llms_txt` — URL to the site's llms.txt
- `mcp` — URL to an MCP server manifest
- `a2a` — URL to an A2A agent card

## Support & Technical

Optional objects for support contact (`email`, `hours`, `response_time`) and technical info (`api_available`, `api_docs_url`, `status_page_url`).

## Extension Mechanism

Discovery manifests MAY include additional fields. Clients MUST ignore unknown fields. Extensions SHOULD use namespaced prefixes (e.g. `x_mcp`, `x_a2a`).

## Minimal Example

```json
{
  "schema_version": "0.1",
  "name": "Your Company",
  "website": "https://example.com",
  "summary": "What you do, in one sentence.",
  "canonical_pages": {
    "home": "https://example.com/",
    "contact": "https://example.com/contact"
  },
  "last_updated": "2026-03-05"
}
```

## Pages on this site

- **Home**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/ — Overview and quick start guide
- **Specification**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/spec — Full specification text (v0.1)
- **Quick Start**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/quickstart — Get set up in under 5 minutes
- **Generate**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/generate — AI prompt, web wizard, or CLI to create your discovery manifest
- **Examples**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/examples — Discovery manifest examples for SaaS, consulting, e-commerce, open source
- **Validator**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/validator — Online playground and CLI to validate discovery manifests
- **FAQ**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/faq — Frequently asked questions
- **Why Not Schema.org?**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/why-not-schema-org — How AI-Me differs from schema.org
- **Roadmap**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/roadmap — Planned features and future versions
- **Governance**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/governance — How the spec evolves
- **Contributing**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/contributing — How to contribute
- **Artifacts**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/artifacts — Downloadable assets (schema, badge)

## Tools

- **Validator CLI**: `npx @ai-me/validator https://yourdomain.com` — validates a site's discovery manifest
- **Generator CLI**: `npx @ai-me/create` — interactive CLI to generate ai-me.json
- **Online Validator**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/validator — paste JSON and validate in browser
- **Web Generator**: https://spec.ai-me.dev/generate — multi-step form to build your discovery manifest

## Schema

JSON Schema URL: https://spec.ai-me.dev/schemas/ai-me.schema.json
Schema draft: JSON Schema draft-07
Validation: Use AJV or any draft-07 compatible validator

## License

- Specification, schemas, and examples: CC-BY 4.0
- Code (website, validator, tooling): Apache-2.0

## Source Code

GitHub: https://github.com/aselims/ai-me
Discovery Manifest: https://spec.ai-me.dev/.well-known/ai-me.json
